am.label package¶

Submodules¶

am.label.label module¶

Match/Mismatch label

class analogical_modeling.am.label.label.Label(l: set[int] | Label, c: int | None = None)¶

Bases: object

This Label implementations stores match and mismatch data in a BitSet.

all_matching() → bool¶

Check whether all attributes match.

descendant_iterator() → Iterator[Label]¶

Create iterator of all descendants of the label.

The “descendants” of a label are the set of labels with the same “mismatch” entries, but with one or more of the “match” entries changed into a “mismatch” entry. For example, the children of {match, mismatch, mismatch, match} are:

  • {mismatch, mismatch, mismatch, match}

  • {match, mismatch, mismatch, mismatch}

  • {mismatch, mismatch, mismatch, mismatch}

intersect(other_label: Label) → Label¶

Create a new label for which each location is marked as a match if both this label and other_label are marked match, otherwise mismatch. In other words, keep all mismatches from both labels.

Parameters:

other_label – the label to intersect with this one

is_descendant_of(possible_ancestor: Label) → bool¶

Determine if this label is the “descendant” of possible_ancestor.

This label is a descendant of the other label if every mismatching entry in the other label is also a mismatching entry in this label. Any label is also a descendant of itself.

Parameters:

possible_ancestor – possible label ancestor

matches(index: int) → bool¶

Determine if the given index is marked as a match or a mismatch.

Parameters:

index – index of the attribute being represented

num_matches() → int¶

Return the total number of matches marked in this label.

union(other: Label) → Label¶

Create a new label for which each location is marked as a match if either this label or other is marked match, otherwise mismatch. In other words, keep all matches from both labels.

Parameters:

other – the other label

class analogical_modeling.am.label.label.SubsetIterator(bitset_label: Label)¶

Bases: object

Construct an iterator over all subsets of this label.

am.label.labeler module¶

Labeler

class analogical_modeling.am.label.labeler.Labeler(test: Instance, ignore_unknowns: bool, ndc: NonspecifiedDataCompare)¶

Bases: object

Analogical Modeling uses labels composed of boolean vectors in order to group instances into subcontexts and subcontexts in supracontexts. Training set instances are assigned labels by comparing them with the instance to be classified and encoding matched attributes and mismatched attributes in a boolean vector.

This class is used to assign context labels to training instances by comparison with the instance being classified.

from_bits(label_bits: int) → Label¶

For testing purposes, this method allows the client to directly specify the label using the bits of an integer.

get_cardinality() → int¶
Returns:

cardinality of the generated labels, or how many instance attributes are considered during labeling

get_context_list(label: Label, mismatch_string: str) → list[str]¶

Return a list representing the context.

If the input test instance attributes are “A C D Z R”, the label is 00101, and the mismatch_string is “*”, then the return list will be “A”, “C”, “*”, “Z”, “*”.

get_context_string(label: Label) → str¶

Returns a string representing the context. If the input test instance attributes are “A C D Z R”, and the label is 00101, then the return string will be “A C * Z *”.

get_instance_atts_string(instance: Instance, att_delimiter: str) → str¶

Return a string containing the attributes of the input instance (minus the class attribute and ignored attributes).

get_instance_atts_values_list(instance: Instance) → list[str]¶

Return a list containing the attributes of the input instance (minus the class attribute and ignored attributes).

get_lattice_bottom() → Label¶
Creates and returns the label which belongs at the bottom of the boolean

lattice formed by the subcontexts labeled by this labeler, i.e. the one for which every feature is a mismatch.

Returns:

label with all mismatches

get_lattice_top() → Label¶

Creates and returns the label which belongs at the top of the boolean lattice formed by the subcontexts labeled by this labeler, i.e. the one for which every feature is a match.

return:

label with all matches

is_ignored(index: int) → bool¶

Check if the attribute at the given index is ignored during labeling.

The default behavior is to ignore the attributes with unknown values in the test instance if self.ignore_unknowns is True.

Parameters:

index – index of the attribute being queried

Returns:

True if the given attribute is ignored during labeling; False otherwise.

label(data: Instance) → Label¶

Create a context label for the input instance by comparing it with the test instance.

The cardinality of the label will be the same as the test and data items. At any given index i, label.Label.matches(i) will return True if that feature is the same in the test and data instances.

Parameters:

data – instance to be labeled

Returns:

the label for the context that the instance belongs to

num_partitions() → int¶
Returns:

number of label partitions available via partition

partition(label: Label, partition_index: int) → Label¶

Create partition for given label.

In distributed processing, it is necessary to split labels into partitions. A full label is partitioned into pieces 0 through num_partitions(), so code to process labels in pieces should look like this:

my_label = MyLabeler.label(my_instance);
for i in MyLabeler.num_partitions():
    process(MyLabeler.partition(my_label, i))
Parameters:

  • label – Label to create partitions for

  • partition_index – index of the partition to return

Returns:

a new label representing a portion of the attributes represented by the input label

Raises:

  • ValueError – if the partitionIndex is greater than num_partitions() or less than zero

  • ValueError – if the input label is not compatible with this labeler

partitions() → list[Partition]¶

This provides a default partitioning implementation which is overridable by child classes.

Returns:

array of partitions indicating how labels can be split into partitions

class analogical_modeling.am.label.labeler.Partition(s: int, l: int)¶

Bases: object

Simple class for storing index spans.

class analogical_modeling.am.label.labeler.Partitioner(s: Partition)¶

Bases: object

Class for storing label partitions.

extract(label: Label) → Label¶

am.label.missing_data_compare module¶

Specification of how to handle non-specified data.

class analogical_modeling.am.label.missing_data_compare.NonspecifiedDataCompare(*values)¶

Bases: Enum

Enum specifying non-specified data comparison.

MATCH = ('match', 'Consider the non-specified attribute value to match anything')¶
MISMATCH = ('mismatch', 'Consider the non-specified attribute value to be a mismatch')¶
VARIABLE = ('variable', 'Treat the the non-specified attribute value as an attribute value of its own; a non-specified value will match another non-specified value, but nothing else.')¶
matches(i1: Instance, i2: Instance, idx: int) → bool¶

Compare the two instances and return the comparison result.

It is assumed that the first instance has a non-specified value for the given attribute.

Parameters:

  • i1 – first instance

  • i2 – second instance

  • idx – index of attribute to be compared between the two instances

Returns:

True if the attributes match, False if they do not; the matching mechanism depends on the chosen algorithm
